name: mailnagger
documentation:
![Mailnagger](https://github.com/tikank/mailnagger/blob/22f00f270c616dc94e9bee301146ff117d00b3a4/data/icons/hicolor/256x256/apps/mailnag.png)

## An extensible mail notification daemon

Mailnagger is a daemon program that checks POP3 and IMAP servers for new mail.
On mail arrival it performs various actions provided by plugins.
Mailnagger comes with a set of desktop-independent default plugins for
visual/sound notifications, script execution etc. and can be extended
with additional plugins easily.

Mailnagger is a fork of [Mailnag](https://github.com/pulb/mailnag).

__This project needs your support!__

If you like Mailnagger, please help to keep it going by [contributing code](https://github.com/tikank/mailnagger),
[reporting/fixing bugs](https://github.com/tikank/mailnagger/issues),
[translating strings into your native language](https://github.com/tikank/mailnagger/tree/master/po),
or [writing docs](https://github.com/tikank/mailnagger/tree/master/docs).


## Installation

Easiest way to install Mailnagger is to use
[pipx](https://pypi.org/project/pipx/).

Run
```
    pipx install mailnagger
```
though make sure the requirements stated below are met.


### Requirements

* python (>= 3.9)
* pygobject
* gir-notify (>= 0.7.6)
* gir-gtk-3.0
* gir-gdkpixbuf-2.0
* gir-glib-2.0
* gir-gst-plugins-base-1.0
* python-dbus
* pyxdg
* gettext
* gir1.2-secret-1 (optional)


## Configuration

Run `mailnagger-config` to setup Mailnagger.

Closing the configuration window will start Mailnagger automatically.

> **Note:**
> Mailnagger uses same configuration files and secret storage as Mailnag.
> They are currently compatible, but there will be someday some kind of
> migration to Mailnagger's own configuration files.


### Default Mail Client

Clicking a mail notification popup will open the default mail client specified in `GNOME Control Center -> Details -> Default Applications`.
If you're a webmail (e.g. gmail) user and want your account to be launched in a browser, please install a tool like [gnome-gmail](http://gnome-gmail.sourceforge.net).


### Desktop Integration

By default, Mailnagger emits libnotify notifications, which work fine on
most desktop environments but are visible for a few seconds only.
If you like to have a tighter desktop integration (e.g. a permanently visible indicator in your top panel) you have to install an appropriate
extension/plugin for your desktop shell.

Mailnag has following desktop extensions:

* GNOME-Shell ([GNOME-Shell extension](https://github.com/pulb/mailnag-gnome-shell)) 
* KDE ([Plasma 5 applet by driglu4it](https://store.kde.org/p/1420222/))
* Cinnamon ([Applet by hyOzd](https://bitbucket.org/hyOzd/mailnagapplet))
* Elementary Pantheon ([MessagingMenu plugin](https://github.com/pulb/mailnag-messagingmenu-plugin))
* XFCE ([MessagingMenu plugin](https://github.com/pulb/mailnag-messagingmenu-plugin))

Since Mailnagger is essentially same as Mailnag, those extensions should/might
work with Mailnagger.

Furthermore, GNOME users can also install the [GOA plugin](https://github.com/pulb/mailnag-goa-plugin),
which makes Mailnagger aware of email accounts specified in GNOME Online Accounts.


### Troubleshooting

__Gmail doesn't work__

If Mailnagger is unable to connect to your Gmail account, please try the following solutions:
* Install the [GOA plugin](https://github.com/pulb/mailnag-goa-plugin) to connect via GNOME online accounts
* Have a look at the [FAQ](https://github.com/pulb/mailnag/wiki/FAQ)
* Try to apply [this](https://github.com/pulb/mailnag/issues/190) workaround

__Other issues__

If Mailnagger doesn't work properly for you, either examine the system log
for errors (`journalctl -b _COMM=mailnagger`)
or run `mailnagger` in a terminal and observe the output.

functions.name: Mailnag.common.subproc.start_subprocess
documentation: Starts a subprocess and an associated thread that waits for
the subprocess to terminate (prevents zombie processes).

functions.name: Mailnag.common.subproc.terminate_subprocesses
documentation: Terminates all subprocesses that were started by
start_subprocess(). Subprocesses that don't terminate
within the timeframe (seconds) specified by the
timeout argument, are sent a kill signal.

This awesome piece of code is intented to encode and decode modified UTF-7 
(the one that is used for IMAP folder names)

encode_mutf7(text) - to encode
decode_mutf7(text) - to decode

classes.name: Mailnag.common.accounts.Account

methods.name: Mailnag.common.accounts.Account.set_config
documentation: Set accounts configuration.

methods.name: Mailnag.common.accounts.Account.get_config
documentation: Return account's configuration as a dict.

methods.name: Mailnag.common.accounts.Account.open
documentation: Open mailbox for the account.

methods.name: Mailnag.common.accounts.Account.close
documentation: Close mailbox for this account.

methods.name: Mailnag.common.accounts.Account.is_open
documentation: Returns true if the mailbox is opened.
Indicates whether the account
holds an active existing connection.

methods.name: Mailnag.common.accounts.Account.list_messages
documentation:
Lists unseen messages from the mailbox for this account.
Yields a set of tuples (folder, message).

methods.name: Mailnag.common.accounts.Account.supports_notifications
documentation: Returns True if account supports notifications.

methods.name: Mailnag.common.accounts.Account.notify_next_change
documentation:
Asks mailbox to notify next change.
Callback is called when new mail arrives or removed.
This may raise an exception if mailbox does not support
notifications.

methods.name: Mailnag.common.accounts.Account.cancel_notifications
documentation:
Cancels notifications.
This may raise an exception if mailbox does not support
notifications.

methods.name: Mailnag.common.accounts.Account.request_server_folders
documentation:
Requests folder names (list) from a server.
Returns an empty list if mailbox does not support folders.

methods.name: Mailnag.common.accounts.Account.get_id
documentation: Returns unique id for the account.
Account class

classes.name: Mailnag.common.plugins.HookTypes
documentation: All known hook types.

classes.name: Mailnag.common.plugins.HookRegistry

methods.name: Mailnag.common.plugins.HookRegistry.register_hook_func
documentation: Priority should be an integer value fom 0 (very high) to 100 (very low)
Plugin hooks will be called in order from high to low priority.
Registry class for plugin hooks.
Registered hook functions must not block the mailnag daemon.
Hook functions with an execution time > 1s should be
implemented non-blocking (i. e. asynchronously).

classes.name: Mailnag.common.plugins.MailnagController

methods.name: Mailnag.common.plugins.MailnagController.get_hooks
documentation: Returns a HookRegistry object.

methods.name: Mailnag.common.plugins.MailnagController.shutdown
documentation: Shuts down the Mailnag process.
May throw an InvalidOperationException.

methods.name: Mailnag.common.plugins.MailnagController.check_for_mails
documentation: Enforces a manual mail check.
May throw an InvalidOperationException.

methods.name: Mailnag.common.plugins.MailnagController.mark_mail_as_read
documentation: Marks the mail with specified mail_id as read.
May throw an InvalidOperationException.
Abstract base class for a MailnagController instance
passed to plugins.

functions.name: Mailnag.backends.create_backend
documentation: Create mailbox backend of specified type and parameters.

functions.name: Mailnag.backends.get_mailbox_parameter_specs
documentation:
Returns mailbox backend specific parameter specification.
The specification is a list objects which have atributes:
* param_name - the name of argument in which parameter value is given to
                                backend constructor
* option_name - the name used in configuration
* from_str - the function to convert string to parameter specific type
* default_value - the value used when the option is not found

classes.name: Mailnag.backends.base.MailboxBackend

methods.name: Mailnag.backends.base.MailboxBackend.__init__
documentation:
Constructor should accept any kind of backend specific
parameters.

methods.name: Mailnag.backends.base.MailboxBackend.open
documentation: Opens the mailbox.

methods.name: Mailnag.backends.base.MailboxBackend.close
documentation: Closes the mailbox.

methods.name: Mailnag.backends.base.MailboxBackend.is_open
documentation: Returns true if mailbox is open.

methods.name: Mailnag.backends.base.MailboxBackend.list_messages
documentation:
Lists unseen messages from the mailbox for this account.
Yields tuples (folder, message, flags) for every message.

methods.name: Mailnag.backends.base.MailboxBackend.request_folders
documentation:
Returns list of folder names available in the mailbox.
Raises an exceptions if mailbox does not support folders.

methods.name: Mailnag.backends.base.MailboxBackend.supports_mark_as_seen
documentation: Returns True if mailbox supports flagging mails as seen.

methods.name: Mailnag.backends.base.MailboxBackend.mark_as_seen
documentation:
Asks mailbox to flag mails in the list as seen.
This may raise an exception if mailbox does not support this action.

methods.name: Mailnag.backends.base.MailboxBackend.supports_notifications
documentation: Returns True if mailbox supports notifications.

methods.name: Mailnag.backends.base.MailboxBackend.notify_next_change
documentation:
Asks mailbox to notify next change.
Callback is called when new mail arrives or a mail is removed.
This may raise an exception if mailbox does not support
notifications.

methods.name: Mailnag.backends.base.MailboxBackend.cancel_notifications
documentation:
Cancels notifications.
This may raise an exception if mailbox does not support
notifications.

Interface for mailbox backends.

Mailbox backend implements access to the specific type of mailbox.

Return path to filename if it exists anywhere in the data paths,
else return None

modules.name: Mailnag.common.imaplib2
documentation:
Threaded IMAP4 client for Python 3.

Based on RFC 3501 and original imaplib module.

Public classes:   IMAP4
                  IMAP4_SSL
                  IMAP4_stream

Public functions: Internaldate2Time
                  ParseFlags
                  Time2Internaldate
!/usr/bin/env python3

functions.name: Mailnag.common.imaplib2.Int2AP
documentation:
string = Int2AP(num)
Return 'num' converted to bytes using characters from the set 'A'..'P'

functions.name: Mailnag.common.imaplib2.Internaldate2Time
documentation:
time_tuple = Internaldate2Time(resp)

Parse an IMAP4 INTERNALDATE string.

Return corresponding local time.  The return value is a
time.struct_time instance or None if the string has wrong format.

functions.name: Mailnag.common.imaplib2.Time2Internaldate
documentation:
'"DD-Mmm-YYYY HH:MM:SS +HHMM"' = Time2Internaldate(date_time)

Convert 'date_time' to IMAP4 INTERNALDATE representation.

The date_time argument can be a number (int or float) representing
seconds since epoch (as returned by time.time()), a 9-tuple
representing local time, an instance of time.struct_time (as
returned by time.localtime()), an aware datetime instance or a
double-quoted string.  In the last case, it is assumed to already
be in the correct format.

functions.name: Mailnag.common.imaplib2.ParseFlags
documentation:
('flag', ...) = ParseFlags(line)
Convert IMAP4 flags response to python tuple.

variables.name: Mailnag.common.imaplib2.IDLE_TIMEOUT
documentation: Don't stay in IDLE state longer

variables.name: Mailnag.common.imaplib2.READ_POLL_TIMEOUT
documentation: Without this timeout interrupted network connections can hang reader

variables.name: Mailnag.common.imaplib2.READ_SIZE
documentation: Consume all available in socket

variables.name: Mailnag.common.imaplib2.DFLT_DEBUG_BUF_LVL
documentation: Level above which the logging output goes directly to stderr

variables.name: Mailnag.common.imaplib2.TLS_SECURE
documentation: Recognised TLS levels

variables.name: Mailnag.common.imaplib2.AllowedVersions
documentation: Most recent first

variables.name: Mailnag.common.imaplib2.Internaldate2tuple
documentation: (Backward compatible)

classes.name: Mailnag.common.imaplib2.Request
documentation: Private class to represent a request awaiting response.

classes.name: Mailnag.common.imaplib2.IMAP4

methods.name: Mailnag.common.imaplib2.IMAP4.open
documentation:
open(host=None, port=None)
Setup connection to remote server on "host:port"
    (default: localhost:standard IMAP4 port).
This connection will be used by the routines:
    read, send, shutdown, socket.
Overridable methods

methods.name: Mailnag.common.imaplib2.IMAP4.open_socket
documentation:
open_socket()
Open socket choosing first address family available.

methods.name: Mailnag.common.imaplib2.IMAP4.start_compressing
documentation:
start_compressing()
Enable deflate compression on the socket (RFC 4978).

methods.name: Mailnag.common.imaplib2.IMAP4.read
documentation:
data = read(size)
Read at most 'size' bytes from remote.

methods.name: Mailnag.common.imaplib2.IMAP4.send
documentation:
send(data)
Send 'data' to remote.

methods.name: Mailnag.common.imaplib2.IMAP4.shutdown
documentation:
shutdown()
Close I/O established in "open".

methods.name: Mailnag.common.imaplib2.IMAP4.socket
documentation:
socket = socket()
Return socket instance used to connect to IMAP4 server.

methods.name: Mailnag.common.imaplib2.IMAP4.enable_compression
documentation:
enable_compression()
Ask the server to start compressing the connection.
Should be called from user of this class after instantiation, as in:
    if 'COMPRESS=DEFLATE' in imapobj.capabilities:
        imapobj.enable_compression()
Utility methods

methods.name: Mailnag.common.imaplib2.IMAP4.pop_untagged_responses
documentation:
for typ,data in pop_untagged_responses(): pass
Generator for any remaining untagged responses.
Returns and removes untagged responses in order of reception.
Use at your own risk!

methods.name: Mailnag.common.imaplib2.IMAP4.recent
documentation:
(typ, [data]) = recent()
Return 'RECENT' responses if any exist,
else prompt server for an update using the 'NOOP' command.
'data' is None if no new messages,
else list of RECENT responses, most recent last.

methods.name: Mailnag.common.imaplib2.IMAP4.response
documentation:
(code, [data]) = response(code)
Return data for response 'code' if received, or None.
Old value for response 'code' is cleared.

methods.name: Mailnag.common.imaplib2.IMAP4.append
documentation:
(typ, [data]) = append(mailbox, flags, date_time, message)
Append message to named mailbox.
All args except `message' can be None.
IMAP4 commands

methods.name: Mailnag.common.imaplib2.IMAP4.authenticate
documentation:
(typ, [data]) = authenticate(mechanism, authobject)
Authenticate command - requires response processing.

'mechanism' specifies which authentication mechanism is to
be used - it must appear in <instance>.capabilities in the
form AUTH=<mechanism>.

'authobject' must be a callable object:

        data = authobject(response)

It will be called to process server continuation responses,
the 'response' argument will be a 'bytes'.  It should return
bytes that will be encoded and sent to server.  It should
return None if the client abort response '*' should be sent
instead.

methods.name: Mailnag.common.imaplib2.IMAP4.capability
documentation:
(typ, [data]) = capability()
Fetch capabilities list from server.

methods.name: Mailnag.common.imaplib2.IMAP4.check
documentation:
(typ, [data]) = check()
Checkpoint mailbox on server.

methods.name: Mailnag.common.imaplib2.IMAP4.close
documentation:
(typ, [data]) = close()
Close currently selected mailbox.

Deleted messages are removed from writable mailbox.
This is the recommended command before 'LOGOUT'.

methods.name: Mailnag.common.imaplib2.IMAP4.copy
documentation:
(typ, [data]) = copy(message_set, new_mailbox)
Copy 'message_set' messages onto end of 'new_mailbox'.

methods.name: Mailnag.common.imaplib2.IMAP4.create
documentation:
(typ, [data]) = create(mailbox)
Create new mailbox.

methods.name: Mailnag.common.imaplib2.IMAP4.delete
documentation:
(typ, [data]) = delete(mailbox)
Delete old mailbox.

methods.name: Mailnag.common.imaplib2.IMAP4.deleteacl
documentation:
(typ, [data]) = deleteacl(mailbox, who)
Delete the ACLs (remove any rights) set for who on mailbox.

methods.name: Mailnag.common.imaplib2.IMAP4.enable
documentation:
Send an RFC5161 enable string to the server.

(typ, [data]) = <intance>.enable(capability)

methods.name: Mailnag.common.imaplib2.IMAP4.examine
documentation:
(typ, [data]) = examine(mailbox='INBOX')
Select a mailbox for READ-ONLY access. (Flushes all untagged responses.)
'data' is count of messages in mailbox ('EXISTS' response).
Mandated responses are ('FLAGS', 'EXISTS', 'RECENT', 'UIDVALIDITY'), so
other responses should be obtained via "response('FLAGS')" etc.

methods.name: Mailnag.common.imaplib2.IMAP4.expunge
documentation:
(typ, [data]) = expunge()
Permanently remove deleted items from selected mailbox.
Generates 'EXPUNGE' response for each deleted message.
'data' is list of 'EXPUNGE'd message numbers in order received.

methods.name: Mailnag.common.imaplib2.IMAP4.fetch
documentation:
(typ, [data, ...]) = fetch(message_set, message_parts)
Fetch (parts of) messages.
'message_parts' should be a string of selected parts
enclosed in parentheses, eg: "(UID BODY[TEXT])".
'data' are tuples of message part envelope and data,
followed by a string containing the trailer.

methods.name: Mailnag.common.imaplib2.IMAP4.getacl
documentation:
(typ, [data]) = getacl(mailbox)
Get the ACLs for a mailbox.

methods.name: Mailnag.common.imaplib2.IMAP4.getannotation
documentation:
(typ, [data]) = getannotation(mailbox, entry, attribute)
Retrieve ANNOTATIONs.

methods.name: Mailnag.common.imaplib2.IMAP4.getquota
documentation:
(typ, [data]) = getquota(root)
Get the quota root's resource usage and limits.
(Part of the IMAP4 QUOTA extension defined in rfc2087.)

methods.name: Mailnag.common.imaplib2.IMAP4.getquotaroot
documentation:
(typ, [[QUOTAROOT responses...], [QUOTA responses...]]) = getquotaroot(mailbox)
Get the list of quota roots for the named mailbox.

methods.name: Mailnag.common.imaplib2.IMAP4.id
documentation:
(typ, [data]) = <instance>.id(kv_pairs)
'kv_pairs' is a possibly empty list of keys and values.
'data' is a list of ID key value pairs or NIL.
NB: a single argument is assumed to be correctly formatted and is passed through unchanged
(for backward compatibility with earlier version).
Exchange information for problem analysis and determination.
The ID extension is defined in RFC 2971.

methods.name: Mailnag.common.imaplib2.IMAP4.idle
documentation:
"(typ, [data]) = idle(timeout=None)
Put server into IDLE mode until server notifies some change,
or 'timeout' (secs) occurs (default: 29 minutes),
or another IMAP4 command is scheduled.

methods.name: Mailnag.common.imaplib2.IMAP4.list
documentation:
(typ, [data]) = list(directory='""', pattern='*')
List mailbox names in directory matching pattern.
'data' is list of LIST responses.

NB: for 'pattern':
% matches all except separator ( so LIST "" "%" returns names at root)
* matches all (so LIST "" "*" returns whole directory tree from root)

methods.name: Mailnag.common.imaplib2.IMAP4.login
documentation:
(typ, [data]) = login(user, password)
Identify client using plaintext password.
NB: 'password' will be quoted.

methods.name: Mailnag.common.imaplib2.IMAP4.login_cram_md5
documentation:
(typ, [data]) = login_cram_md5(user, password)
Force use of CRAM-MD5 authentication.

methods.name: Mailnag.common.imaplib2.IMAP4._CRAM_MD5_AUTH
documentation: Authobject to use with CRAM-MD5 authentication.

methods.name: Mailnag.common.imaplib2.IMAP4.logout
documentation:
(typ, [data]) = logout()
Shutdown connection to server.
Returns server 'BYE' response.
NB: You must call this to shut down threads before discarding an instance.

methods.name: Mailnag.common.imaplib2.IMAP4.lsub
documentation:
(typ, [data, ...]) = lsub(directory='""', pattern='*')
List 'subscribed' mailbox names in directory matching pattern.
'data' are tuples of message part envelope and data.

methods.name: Mailnag.common.imaplib2.IMAP4.myrights
documentation:
(typ, [data]) = myrights(mailbox)
Show my ACLs for a mailbox (i.e. the rights that I have on mailbox).

methods.name: Mailnag.common.imaplib2.IMAP4.namespace
documentation:
(typ, [data, ...]) = namespace()
Returns IMAP namespaces ala rfc2342.

methods.name: Mailnag.common.imaplib2.IMAP4.noop
documentation:
(typ, [data]) = noop()
Send NOOP command.

methods.name: Mailnag.common.imaplib2.IMAP4.partial
documentation:
(typ, [data, ...]) = partial(message_num, message_part, start, length)
Fetch truncated part of a message.
'data' is tuple of message part envelope and data.
NB: obsolete.

methods.name: Mailnag.common.imaplib2.IMAP4.proxyauth
documentation:
(typ, [data]) = proxyauth(user)
Assume authentication as 'user'.
(Allows an authorised administrator to proxy into any user's mailbox.)

methods.name: Mailnag.common.imaplib2.IMAP4.rename
documentation:
(typ, [data]) = rename(oldmailbox, newmailbox)
Rename old mailbox name to new.

methods.name: Mailnag.common.imaplib2.IMAP4.search
documentation:
(typ, [data]) = search(charset, criterion, ...)
Search mailbox for matching messages.
If UTF8 is enabled, charset MUST be None.
'data' is space separated list of matching message numbers.

methods.name: Mailnag.common.imaplib2.IMAP4.select
documentation:
(typ, [data]) = select(mailbox='INBOX', readonly=False)
Select a mailbox. (Flushes all untagged responses.)
'data' is count of messages in mailbox ('EXISTS' response).
Mandated responses are ('FLAGS', 'EXISTS', 'RECENT', 'UIDVALIDITY'), so
other responses should be obtained via "response('FLAGS')" etc.

methods.name: Mailnag.common.imaplib2.IMAP4.setacl
documentation:
(typ, [data]) = setacl(mailbox, who, what)
Set a mailbox acl.

methods.name: Mailnag.common.imaplib2.IMAP4.setannotation
documentation:
(typ, [data]) = setannotation(mailbox[, entry, attribute]+)
Set ANNOTATIONs.

methods.name: Mailnag.common.imaplib2.IMAP4.setquota
documentation:
(typ, [data]) = setquota(root, limits)
Set the quota root's resource limits.

methods.name: Mailnag.common.imaplib2.IMAP4.sort
documentation:
(typ, [data]) = sort(sort_criteria, charset, search_criteria, ...)
IMAP4rev1 extension SORT command.

methods.name: Mailnag.common.imaplib2.IMAP4.starttls

(typ, [data]) = starttls(keyfile=None, certfile=None, ca_certs=None, cert_verify_cb=None, ssl_version="ssl23", tls_level="tls_compat")
Start TLS negotiation as per RFC 2595.

methods.name: Mailnag.common.imaplib2.IMAP4.status
documentation:
(typ, [data]) = status(mailbox, names)
Request named status conditions for mailbox.

methods.name: Mailnag.common.imaplib2.IMAP4.store
documentation:
(typ, [data]) = store(message_set, command, flags)
Alters flag dispositions for messages in mailbox.

methods.name: Mailnag.common.imaplib2.IMAP4.subscribe
documentation:
(typ, [data]) = subscribe(mailbox)
Subscribe to new mailbox.

methods.name: Mailnag.common.imaplib2.IMAP4.thread
documentation:
(type, [data]) = thread(threading_alogrithm, charset, search_criteria, ...)
IMAPrev1 extension THREAD command.

methods.name: Mailnag.common.imaplib2.IMAP4.uid
documentation:
(typ, [data]) = uid(command, arg, ...)
Execute "command arg ..." with messages identified by UID,
    rather than message number.
Assumes 'command' is legal in current state.
Returns response appropriate to 'command'.

methods.name: Mailnag.common.imaplib2.IMAP4.unsubscribe
documentation:
(typ, [data]) = unsubscribe(mailbox)
Unsubscribe from old mailbox.

methods.name: Mailnag.common.imaplib2.IMAP4.xatom
documentation:
(typ, [data]) = xatom(name, arg, ...)
Allow simple extension commands notified by server in CAPABILITY response.
Assumes extension command 'name' is legal in current state.
Returns response appropriate to extension command 'name'.

methods.name: Mailnag.common.imaplib2.IMAP4._append_untagged
documentation: Internal methods

methods.name: Mailnag.common.imaplib2.IMAP4._close_threads
documentation: Threads

methods.name: Mailnag.common.imaplib2.IMAP4._reader
documentation: No "poll" - use select()

Threaded IMAP4 client class.

Instantiate with:
    IMAP4(host=None, port=None, debug=None, debug_file=None, identifier=None, timeout=None, debug_buf_lvl=None)

    host          - host's name (default: localhost);
    port          - port number (default: standard IMAP4 port);
    debug         - debug level (default: 0 - no debug);
    debug_file    - debug stream (default: sys.stderr);
    identifier    - thread identifier prefix (default: host);
    timeout       - timeout in seconds when expecting a command response (default: no timeout),
    debug_buf_lvl - debug level at which buffering is turned off.

All IMAP4rev1 commands are supported by methods of the same name.

Each command returns a tuple: (type, [data, ...]) where 'type'
is usually 'OK' or 'NO', and 'data' is either the text from the
tagged response, or untagged results from command. Each 'data' is
either a string, or a tuple. If a tuple, then the first part is the
header of the response, and the second part contains the data (ie:
'literal' value).

Errors raise the exception class <instance>.error("<reason>").
IMAP4 server errors raise <instance>.abort("<reason>"), which is
a sub-class of 'error'. Mailbox status changes from READ-WRITE to
READ-ONLY raise the exception class <instance>.readonly("<reason>"),
which is a sub-class of 'abort'.

"error" exceptions imply a program error.
"abort" exceptions imply the connection should be reset, and
        the command re-tried.
"readonly" exceptions imply the command should be re-tried.

All commands take two optional named arguments:
    'callback' and 'cb_arg'
If 'callback' is provided then the command is asynchronous, so after
the command is queued for transmission, the call returns immediately
with the tuple (None, None).
The result will be posted by invoking "callback" with one arg, a tuple:
    callback((result, cb_arg, None))
or, if there was a problem:
    callback((None, cb_arg, (exception class, reason)))

Otherwise the command is synchronous (waits for result). But note
that state-changing commands will both block until previous commands
have completed, and block subsequent commands until they have finished.

All (non-callback) string arguments to commands are converted to bytes,
except for AUTHENTICATE, and the last argument to APPEND which is
passed as an IMAP4 literal.  NB: the 'password' argument to the LOGIN
command is always quoted.

There is one instance variable, 'state', that is useful for tracking
whether the client needs to login to the server. If it has the
value "AUTH" after instantiating the class, then the connection
is pre-authenticated (otherwise it will be "NONAUTH"). Selecting a
mailbox changes the state to be "SELECTED", closing a mailbox changes
back to "AUTH", and once the client has logged out, the state changes
to "LOGOUT" and no further commands may be issued.

Note: to use this module, you must read the RFCs pertaining to the
IMAP4 protocol, as the semantics of the arguments to each IMAP4
command are left to the invoker, not to mention the results. Also,
most IMAP servers implement a sub-set of the commands available here.

Note also that you must call logout() to shut down threads before
discarding an instance.

classes.name: Mailnag.common.imaplib2.IMAP4_SSL

methods.name: Mailnag.common.imaplib2.IMAP4_SSL.open
documentation:
open(host=None, port=None)
Setup secure connection to remote server on "host:port"
    (default: localhost:standard IMAP4 SSL port).
This connection will be used by the routines:
    read, send, shutdown, socket, ssl.

methods.name: Mailnag.common.imaplib2.IMAP4_SSL.read
documentation:
data = read(size)
Read at most 'size' bytes from remote.

methods.name: Mailnag.common.imaplib2.IMAP4_SSL.send
documentation:
send(data)
Send 'data' to remote.

methods.name: Mailnag.common.imaplib2.IMAP4_SSL.ssl
documentation:
ssl = ssl()
Return ssl instance used to communicate with the IMAP4 server.

classes.name: Mailnag.common.imaplib2.IMAP4_stream

methods.name: Mailnag.common.imaplib2.IMAP4_stream.open
documentation:
open(host=None, port=None)
Setup a stream connection via 'self.command'.
This connection will be used by the routines:
    read, send, shutdown, socket.

methods.name: Mailnag.common.imaplib2.IMAP4_stream.read
documentation: Read 'size' bytes from remote.

methods.name: Mailnag.common.imaplib2.IMAP4_stream.send
documentation: Send data to remote.

methods.name: Mailnag.common.imaplib2.IMAP4_stream.shutdown
documentation: Close I/O established in "open".
